rubric.description: Designs reusable, composable components with clear props, sensible defaults, and well-managed boundaries between stateful and presentational layers.
Chooses appropriate state strategies (local, lifted, derived, server) and reasons about caching, invalidation, and synchronisation between client and server state.
Identifies rendering and network bottlenecks, applies measurement-driven optimisation (memoisation, code splitting, virtualisation), and understands browser fundamentals.
Explains technical decisions clearly, asks clarifying questions, and structures answers so a non-frontend listener can follow.
Considers end-user impact, accessibility, and edge cases; balances polish and shipping speed; treats UX as a first-class concern.

items.prompt: Walk me through a frontend project you're proud of. What was your role, and what were the most interesting technical decisions?
followUpHints: Push for specifics on what THEY built vs. the team
Ask why they chose the framework/state lib they did
Probe for what they would do differently with hindsight

items.prompt: You're designing a reusable Modal component for a design system. What does its API look like, and how do you keep it flexible without becoming a kitchen sink?
followUpHints: Ask about controlled vs. uncontrolled open state
Probe for handling of focus trapping and the escape key
Push on slotting / composition vs. flag props

items.prompt: In a React app, when would you reach for a server-state library like React Query or SWR versus putting data into a global store like Redux or Zustand? Where do you draw the line?
followUpHints: Ask about cache invalidation strategy
Probe for handling of optimistic updates
Ask how they'd structure a feature that needs both

items.prompt: A page in your app feels janky on mid-tier devices. How do you investigate, and what's the order of operations you'd follow before changing any code?
followUpHints: Push them to mention the Performance panel and flame charts
Ask about the difference between TBT, INP, and FPS
Probe for how they'd measure before AND after a fix

items.prompt: Tell me about a time you used memoisation (React.memo, useMemo, useCallback) to fix a rendering issue. How did you confirm it actually helped?
followUpHints: Probe for whether the fix was the simplest option
Ask if state structure changes would have removed the need
Push for evidence: profiler before/after, not vibes

items.prompt: How do you make sure a complex interactive component, like a custom select or combobox, is accessible to keyboard and screen-reader users?
followUpHints: Ask which ARIA roles/attributes apply
Probe for testing approach (axe, manual NVDA/VoiceOver, etc.)
Ask about focus management on open/close

items.prompt: How do you handle the loading, error, and empty states for a data-heavy view? Walk me through your default playbook.
followUpHints: Push on skeleton vs. spinner trade-offs
Ask about retry UX and what they show the user on a 5xx
Probe for handling of stale-while-revalidate

items.prompt: Describe a time you disagreed with a designer or PM about a UI decision. How did the conversation go, and what was the outcome?
followUpHints: Probe for whether they actually changed their mind, or pushed through
Ask what data or evidence they brought to the discussion
Push on the long-term relationship: was there fallout?

items.prompt: When would you reach for server-side rendering or React Server Components versus a fully client-rendered SPA? What changes about how you write components?
followUpHints: Probe for hydration cost awareness
Ask how they'd handle a feature that needs both
Push on what's hardest about RSC mental model

items.prompt: Tell me about a time you had to ship something faster than felt comfortable. What did you cut, and what did you refuse to cut?
followUpHints: Ask what shipped to users vs. what was deferred
Probe for the framework they used to decide
Push on whether the cuts came back to bite them
